records.ety: [F. proroger, L. prorogare, prorogatum; pro forward + rogare to ask, to ask one for his opinion or vote, or about a law. See Rogation.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Prorogued (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Proroguing (?).]

defs: To protract; to prolong; to extend.
To defer; to delay; to postpone; as, to proroguedeath; to prorogue a marriage.
To end the session of a parliament by an order of the sovereign, thus deferring its business.
quotes: Parliament was prorogued to [meet at] Westminster.
The Parliament was again prorogued to a distant day.
